title: tetrad-lens span attribute schema v1
description: McLuhan tetrad (Laws of Media, 1988) shaped attribute schema for AI agent traces. Encoded as OpenTelemetry span attributes under the tetrad.* namespace, plus an Engelbart H-LAM/T augmentation level under engelbart.*. tetrad.figure_ground is derived from the four scores and is read-only.

tetrad.schema_version.description: Semver of this schema. v0.x = preview, v1.x = stable. Producers MUST set this so consumers can apply migrations.

tetrad.enhance.description: Score in [0, 1] for the law of enhancement. Question (McLuhan 1988): 'What does the artifact enhance, amplify, or intensify?' Multiple laws can co-exist on the same span (orthogonal per the original).

tetrad.enhance.rationale.description: Free-text rationale produced by the tagger (heuristic / LLM-assisted / human). Required when the score is >= 0.5.

tetrad.obsolesce.description: Score in [0, 1] for the law of obsolescence. Question: 'What does it render obsolete or displace?'

tetrad.retrieve.description: Score in [0, 1] for the law of retrieval. Question: 'What does it recover that was previously obsolesced?'

tetrad.reverse.description: Score in [0, 1] for the law of reversal. Question: 'When pushed to its limits, what does it reverse into?'

tetrad.figure_ground.description: DERIVED, read-only. Computed from the four scores by the consumer (e.g., dashboard). Producers MUST NOT set this directly; it is documented here only so consumers agree on the enumeration. Rough rule (subject to revision): figure = enhance+retrieve dominant; ground = obsolesce+reverse dominant; both = all four high; unclear = all four low.

tetrad.tier.description: Which tagger tier produced these scores. annotation always wins over llm, llm always wins over heuristic when the same span is re-scored.

tetrad.confidence.description: Reliability score for the tagger output. Tier 2 must populate this from Judge Reliability Harness (arXiv 2603.05399) or equivalent. Tier 3 (annotation) may set 1.0.

engelbart.level.description: Engelbart H-LAM/T (1962 SRI report) augmentation level extended with the Bootstrap Institute ABC framing (1990s). A = improving the work, B = improving the way we improve the work, C = improving the way we improve the way we improve the work. NOT part of the 1962 paper; the ABC framing is a Bootstrap-era addition, cite accordingly.

engelbart.role_split.description: Optional secondary axis: distribution of work between human and AI on this span. Sums to <= 1.0; remainder is unattributed.

$comment: All attribute names use dots as separators per OpenTelemetry semantic conventions. The tetrad.* and engelbart.* namespaces are not yet reserved with the OTel SIG; do not use the otel.* prefix here (it is reserved). Dual-emit alongside openinference-semantic-conventions and OTel GenAI is opt-in via the OTEL_SEMCONV_STABILITY_OPT_IN env var (see Layer 2 SDK docs).
